title: Bootstrap a license policy for a repository
description: Define an allowlist/denylist of licenses and wire it into CI so new dependencies are gated.
body: Steps: 1. Decide the distribution model (proprietary binary, SaaS, OSS library) since it sets which classes are acceptable. 2. Write an allowlist (typically permissive + weak-copyleft for proprietary) and a denylist (strong/network copyleft, source-available). 3. Encode it in a tool config (cargo-deny, license-checker policy, FOSSA). 4. Wire the check into CI so a denied or unknown license fails the build. 5. Document an exception path that requires human sign-off. Rollback point: before enabling the CI gate. Validation: adding a known-denied dependency fails CI; adding an allowlisted one passes.
